subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: dy/dx = (3-y)cos x — Slope Field, Tangent Line Approx & Separation of Variables
stimulus: Consider the differential equation $\frac{dy}{dx} = (3-y)\cos x$. Let $y = f(x)$ be the particular solution to the differential equation with the initial condition $f(0) = 1$. The function $f$ is defined for all real numbers.

parts.prompt: A portion of the slope field of the differential equation is given. Sketch the solution curve through the point $(0, 1)$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Solution curve passes through $(0, 1)$, follows slope field lines, and approaches $y = 3$ asymptotically
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Curve must pass through $(0,1)$ and follow slope field. The equilibrium solution is $y = 3$; curves starting below $y = 3$ approach it from below.

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the solution curve in part (a) at the point $(0, 1)$. Use the equation to approximate $f(0.2)$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Slope $= (3-1)\cos(0) = 2$. Tangent line: $y = 2x + 1$. Approximation: $f(0.2) \approx 1.4$.

parts.prompt: Find $y = f(x)$, the particular solution to the differential equation with the initial condition $f(0) = 1$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Includes constant of integration and uses initial condition $f(0) = 1$: $-\ln(2) = 0 + C \Rightarrow C = -\ln 2$
1 pt: Solves for $y$: $-\ln|3-y| = \sin x - \ln 2$; $\ln|3-y| = -\sin x + \ln 2$; $|3-y| = 2e^{-\sin x}$; since $f(0) = 1 < 3$: $3-y = 2e^{-\sin x}$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\frac{dy}{3-y} = \cos x\, dx$. $-\ln|3-y| = \sin x + C$. Using $f(0)=1$: $C = -\ln 2$. Since $y < 3$: $3 - y = 2e^{-\sin x}$, so $y = 3 - 2e^{-\sin x}$. Valid for all real $x$. 0/6 if no separation; max 3/6 if no constant of integration.
